meta.title: Further Reflections on Leaving the Eastern Orthodox Church, Part 2
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund (YouTube channel; Joshua Shooping speaking)

questions.question: According to the speaker, his main critique of Eastern Orthodoxy on exclusivity targets what?
options: A particular sectarian form tied to Orthodox ecclesiology (with parallels he draws to Roman Catholicism)
The idea that only baptized people can take communion
The mere fact that Orthodoxy draws any boundaries at all
Liturgical preference over low-church Protestant worship
questions.answer: A particular sectarian form tied to Orthodox ecclesiology (with parallels he draws to Roman Catholicism)

questions.question: What metaphor does the speaker use for diverse visions of what Eastern Orthodoxy "is"?
options: A cathedral with one spire
An inkblot / Rorschach test
A river with one mouth
A mosaic with a single center
questions.answer: An inkblot / Rorschach test

questions.question: Under Theodosius (as described), what legal-religious situation does the speaker highlight?
options: The church was driven back into house churches by persecution
Christianity became optional for citizens
Christianity became the only legal religion; good citizenship and being Christian were legally intertwined
Paganism was restored as the state cult
questions.answer: Christianity became the only legal religion; good citizenship and being Christian were legally intertwined

questions.question: The speaker invokes "the rule of prayer becomes the rule of faith" to argue what?
options: Liturgy never changes
That Scripture replaced liturgy after the Reformation
That only monks determine doctrine
That worship and belief co-develop, undermining a simple "nothing changed" continuity claim across centuries
questions.answer: That worship and belief co-develop, undermining a simple "nothing changed" continuity claim across centuries

questions.question: Which pairing does the speaker associate with fourth-century liturgical development?
options: Monastic rite and cathedral rite emerging and later blending
House churches and underground catacombs only
Byzantine chant and Gregorian chant as identical
Synagogue rite and Quaker silence
questions.answer: Monastic rite and cathedral rite emerging and later blending

questions.question: After Nicaea and the second ecumenical council, which community's breakaway does the speaker mention?
options: The Anabaptists
The Assyrian Church of the East
The Waldensians
The Hussites
questions.answer: The Assyrian Church of the East

questions.question: Regarding Chalcedon, what does the speaker claim about Oriental Orthodox churches and Orthodox canonical expectations?
options: They are automatically considered fully Orthodox without conditions
They are identical to Eastern Orthodoxy in every doctrinal detail
They would have to accept Chalcedon and subsequent ecumenical councils (major and minor decisions) to be considered real Christians on that standard
They rejected only baptism, not councils
questions.answer: They would have to accept Chalcedon and subsequent ecumenical councils (major and minor decisions) to be considered real Christians on that standard

questions.question: How does the speaker characterize the authority of ecumenical councils for many Orthodox (as he describes it)?
options: Rejected entirely after the seventh council
Subordinate only to local parish custom
Purely historical documents with no doctrinal weight
Functionally equivalent to Scripture (or nearly so, depending on whom you ask)
questions.answer: Functionally equivalent to Scripture (or nearly so, depending on whom you ask)

questions.question: In the logical chain the speaker attributes to Orthodox ecclesiology, what follows from being outside the visible church?
options: One de facto lacks the Spirit of Christ and thus is not truly Christian
One might still have the Spirit of Christ
One is in schism but necessarily saved
One is only barred from ordination
questions.answer: One de facto lacks the Spirit of Christ and thus is not truly Christian

questions.question: Which Protestant author does the speaker cite as affirming that non-Presbyterians are not necessarily outside the church?
options: Martin Luther
Francis Turretin
John Knox
John Calvin
questions.answer: Francis Turretin

questions.question: How does the speaker contrast Presbyterian exclusivity with the exclusivity he attributes to Eastern Orthodoxy?
options: Presbyterians forbid reading the church fathers
Presbyterianism denies baptism to all outsiders
Excluding someone from Presbyterianism does not mean excluding them from salvation or the normal possibility of salvation
Presbyterians teach that only Presbyterians have the Holy Spirit
questions.answer: Excluding someone from Presbyterianism does not mean excluding them from salvation or the normal possibility of salvation

questions.question: Which elements does the speaker list as typical marks of whether the one holy catholic apostolic church is manifesting in a visible church?
options: Apostolic succession paperwork, Latin mass, and pilgrimages
Icons, incense, seven mysteries, and patriarchal blessing
Speaking in tongues, faith healing, and television ministry
Gospel preached (including penal substitution and union with Christ), baptism and Eucharist administered biblically, and proper church discipline
questions.answer: Gospel preached (including penal substitution and union with Christ), baptism and Eucharist administered biblically, and proper church discipline

questions.question: In the hospital chaplaincy story, which traditions did the speaker say he worked alongside?
options: Eastern Orthodox, charismatic Episcopal, Baptist, and Seventh-day Adventist pastors
Jewish and Muslim chaplains only
Roman Catholic and Anglican only
Only other Eastern Orthodox clergy
questions.answer: Eastern Orthodox, charismatic Episcopal, Baptist, and Seventh-day Adventist pastors

questions.question: What New Testament passage does the speaker use against sectarian party spirit?
options: Jesus before Pilate
Paul's criticism in 1 Corinthians about being "of Paul," "of Apollos," "of Cephas," or "of Christ"
The calling of the twelve in Mark
The Sermon on the Mount
questions.answer: Paul's criticism in 1 Corinthians about being "of Paul," "of Apollos," "of Cephas," or "of Christ"

questions.question: How does the speaker respond to being called an apostate?
options: He embraces the label as meaning damned
He denies ever having been Orthodox
He says he does not mind apostasizing from a sect to be true to Christ, but not from Christ
He says apostasy is impossible for any baptized person
questions.answer: He says he does not mind apostasizing from a sect to be true to Christ, but not from Christ

questions.question: What does the speaker claim an Orthodox Christian cannot truly say to a Baptist or Dutch Reformed person, in his view?
options: "You are baptized"
"You should read the fathers"
"You should study church history"
"You're a brother in Christ"
questions.answer: "You're a brother in Christ"

questions.question: The speaker argues that Protestant denominational boundaries differ from Orthodox exclusivism because Protestants can do what?
options: Exclude from a denomination while still calling someone a brother in Christ
Reject the Trinity
Avoid all doctrinal standards
Deny the need for any visible church
questions.answer: Exclude from a denomination while still calling someone a brother in Christ

questions.question: Which emperor begins the narrative of moving Christian worship into large public buildings after persecution eased?
options: Diocletian
Constantine
Nero
Julian the Apostate
questions.answer: Constantine

questions.question: What does the speaker say often happened when he checked Orthodox apologetic claims that "Protestants teach X"?
options: He found Protestant teaching matched the caricature every time
He concluded Orthodox apologists never make historical claims
He almost invariably found it was not the case when he investigated
He stopped reading Protestant sources
questions.answer: He almost invariably found it was not the case when he investigated

questions.question: Which thinkers does the speaker name as examples of Protestants who are not mere "private" readers of Scripture?
options: Only Radical Reformers with no confessional tradition
Only late twentieth-century televangelists
Socrates, Plato, and Aristotle
Figures such as Bavinck, Turretin, Chemnitz, Gerhard, Wesley, Edwards, Owen, Boston, and Goodwin
questions.answer: Figures such as Bavinck, Turretin, Chemnitz, Gerhard, Wesley, Edwards, Owen, Boston, and Goodwin

questions.question: At the end, the speaker clarifies he affirms what about church boundaries?
options: Each church has a right and necessity to clarify its boundaries; he rejects exclusivism that ties salvation to one denomination
Only Orthodox bishops may discipline
No church should ever define membership or doctrine
Visible church boundaries are irrelevant
questions.answer: Each church has a right and necessity to clarify its boundaries; he rejects exclusivism that ties salvation to one denomination

questions.question: The speaker describes the Latin/Western rite relative to the Eastern rite as:
options: A medieval invention with no ancient roots
Equally ancient (if not more ancient in some cases) yet in many ways radically different in how it strikes the senses
Forbidden after Chalcedon
Identical in every liturgical detail
questions.answer: Equally ancient (if not more ancient in some cases) yet in many ways radically different in how it strikes the senses

questions.question: What problem does the speaker raise when both Roman Catholics and Eastern Orthodox claim to be the one true church?
options: That both deny ecumenical councils
That both communions agree on papal infallibility
Who gets to adjudicate—who "plays pope" to say which claim is right
That neither cares about baptism
questions.answer: Who gets to adjudicate—who "plays pope" to say which claim is right

questions.question: Examples the speaker gives of things the early church would not have recognized as part of a simple unchanged package include:
options: Only the use of the Nicene Creed
Celebrating Easter
Reading the Gospels in Greek
The monastic schedule of services and exclusively celibate/unmarried bishops, among other changes
questions.answer: The monastic schedule of services and exclusively celibate/unmarried bishops, among other changes
